title: Fixed-parameter indicators are regime-blind. Here's how I fixed the SuperTrend
author: powerade-trader
selftext:
Backtested Standard SuperTrend (3x, ATR 14) across 3 regimes: bull trend, bear trend, and chop. In trending conditions: Sharpe \~1.1, decent. In chop: Sharpe -0.7, destroyed. The problem isn't the SuperTrend logic — it's the fixed parameter. A choppy market needs a wider band; a trending market benefits from a tighter one. So I built an adaptive version that calculates a 4-factor regime score every bar and linearly interpolates the ATR multiplier from 2.0 (pure trend) to 5.0 (pure chop). Same logic, adaptive sensitivity. The backtest difference is significant.

Here is the Indicator TradingView Page: [https://www.tradingview.com/script/SQprvq8J-Regime-Adaptive-Trend-Engine-AlgoPoint/](https://www.tradingview.com/script/SQprvq8J-Regime-Adaptive-Trend-Engine-AlgoPoint/)
